wizard.configureSubtitle: Choose which agents to install and how to run them.

complete.configured: {{projectName}} is now configured with AI-powered development workflows. Your specialized agents and commands are ready to use.
complete.learnMore: Learn how to get the most out of specrails:

jira.title: Connect a Jira board
jira.subtitle: Optional — back this project's specs with a Jira board. You can also do it later in Settings.

error.title: Setup failed
error.prereqMissing: SpecRails setup needs Node.js, npm, npx and Git available on PATH before it can install this project.
error.prereqRetryHint: Install the missing tools, restart Specrails, then retry setup.

addProject.title: Add Project
addProject.description: Register a project directory to manage it from Specrails.
addProject.noProviderDetected: No AI CLI detected. Install Claude Code or Codex CLI first.
addProject.pathHint: Absolute path to the project root
addProject.multiProviderHint: Both engines will be set up. The first is the project default. Cannot be changed after creation.
addProject.singleProviderHint: Cannot be changed after the project is created.
addProject.toolsRequired_one: {{tools}} is required to add a project
addProject.toolsRequired_other: {{tools}} are required to add a project

errors.selectProvider: Select at least one AI provider

prerequisites.failedToExecuteAt: found at {{path}} but failed to execute
prerequisites.failedToExecuteAtWithError: found at {{path}} but failed to execute ({{error}})
prerequisites.verifyError: Could not verify developer tools locally — install will validate.
prerequisites.reinstallHint: Reinstall the Specrails app to restore bundled tools.
prerequisites.installMissingHint: Install the missing tools to continue.

instructions.title: Install developer tools
instructions.description: SpecRails needs Node.js, npm, npx, and Git on PATH. Pick the snippet for your OS, run it, then click "I installed it, recheck".

darwin.intro: Homebrew gets you both tools in one line. If you do not have brew, use the official downloads.
darwin.installNodeGit: Install Node.js and Git via Homebrew

win32.intro: Winget ships with Windows 11 and recent Windows 10. Open PowerShell or Terminal and run:
win32.note: After install, restart Specrails so Windows refreshes PATH.

corrupted.title: App bundle corrupted
corrupted.description: One or more bundled tools (Node.js, Git) could not be verified.
corrupted.body: This tool is bundled with the Specrails app and cannot be installed separately. If you see this message, the app bundle may be corrupted. Please reinstall Specrails.

setupChat.title: Setup assistant
setupChat.subtitle: Respond to prompts to configure your project
setupChat.keyboardHint: Enter to send, Shift+Enter for new line

welcome.title: Welcome to <spec>spec</spec><rails>rails</rails>
welcome.description: Specrails manages multiple projects from a single interface. Add your first project to get started.
welcome.jiraHint: Connect each project to its own Jira board — specs stay in sync, both ways.

language.title: Choose your language
language.subtitle: Specrails speaks your language
language.intro: Pick the language you want the interface in. It applies instantly — this tour included.
language.note: You can change it any time in Settings → Language.

theme.title: Pick your look
theme.subtitle: Built-in themes, applied instantly
theme.intro: Choose how Specrails should look — the theme applies the moment you click.
theme.note: You can change it any time in Settings → Appearance.

welcome.title: Welcome to Specrails
welcome.subtitle: Your local cockpit for shipping software with AI agents
welcome.intro: Specrails turns an idea into shipped code — and lets you see, steer, and trust every step. Draft a spec by talking to an AI, drop it on an execution rail, and hit Play. The pipeline handles architecture, implementation, review and the pull request, streaming its work live the whole way.
welcome.flowCaption: Each spec flows through Architect → Developer → Reviewer → Ship automatically.
welcome.localNote: 100% local · single user · no accounts. Everything runs on your machine. Your code never leaves the laptop unless you spawn an agent against it.
welcome.tourNote: This quick tour walks through everything the app can do — about a minute.

authorSpecs.title: Turn ideas into specs
authorSpecs.subtitle: Six ways to capture exactly what you want built
authorSpecs.intro: A spec is a description of work you want done. Author one however suits the moment — from a quick one-liner to a full conversation with the AI.
authorSpecs.exploreBody: Describe what you want in a chat; a live draft rebuilds itself every turn. A preset slider tunes how much project context the AI sees — from your message only, up to the full codebase plus your MCP servers.
authorSpecs.quickBody: Already know what you want? Generate a complete spec in a single turn. Optionally enrich it with a Contract Layer: exact names, data shapes, invariants and a file-touch list so the agents don't reinvent anything.
authorSpecs.websiteBody: Opens an embedded browser. Hover-select an element or drag a rectangle, and the screenshot, DOM and applied CSS become attachments. The desktop build ships its own Chromium, so it works offline.
authorSpecs.smashBody: Explode a large spec into a family of smaller sub-specs in one click — each child carries a short summary on its card.
authorSpecs.draftsBody: Park an in-progress exploration as a draft ticket and resume it later; reopen any spec back in Explore to keep refining it.
authorSpecs.compareBody: Drag a spec to the screen edge and a picker of your other specs slides in — review two of them together, tablet-style.

rails.title: Run the pipeline on rails
rails.subtitle: Organize specs into lanes, then launch the agents
rails.intro: The right side of the Dashboard is your Rails — execution lanes. Drag a spec card onto a rail and press ▶ Play to launch an implementation job.
rails.flowCaption: Every phase streams its logs live as it works.
rails.modeBody: Each rail chooses a mode: Implement (the full structured pipeline), Batch (several specs in sequence), or Ultracode (Claude works autonomously, no fixed pipeline).
rails.profilesBody: A per-project, declarative catalog that decides which agents run and which model each one uses — picked per rail and snapshotted per job so concurrent work stays isolated.
rails.jobDetailBody: Every job shows a ticket-identity header, a live duration ticker and running turn/token counts, with the authoritative cost resolved on exit.
rails.isolationBody: Each project has its own database and job queue. Run different projects at the same time and switch between them from the left sidebar — your place is remembered per project.

providers.title: Bring your own agent
providers.subtitle: Claude Code and Codex, as first-class engines
providers.intro: The app treats Claude Code and Codex CLI as interchangeable engines behind one contract. A project can install one or both — the choice is made at install time.
providers.calloutBody: Pick the engine per spec, per rail, or per terminal launch. The app remembers your last choice per project. Codex cost is estimated from a local rate-card and always flagged with a ~.

cost.title: Track every cent
cost.subtitle: Every billable invocation, recorded and visualized
cost.intro: Every AI invocation — across rails, Quick, Explore, AI edits, SMASH and file summaries — is recorded for both Claude and Codex. No more guessing what the agents cost.
cost.analyticsBody: A burn-rate hero, daily timeline, top tickets, model breakdown and a cost-vs-turns scatter. Filter by period (7d / 30d / 90d / All) and by surface.
cost.perTicketBody: Each spec's detail shows what it cost and how many turns it took, deep-linking into Analytics filtered to that ticket.
cost.honestBody: Claude cost is the provider-billed figure; Codex is estimated and flagged with <mono>~</mono>. Token totals include the cache tiers, so the figures actually reconcile.
cost.budgetsBody: Set a daily budget and a per-job cost alert; the app auto-pauses the queue when you cross the line. Export anything to CSV or JSON.

workspace.title: Make it your workspace
workspace.subtitle: Everything you need without leaving the window
workspace.terminalBody: A real VS-Code-style terminal at the bottom (<mod>{{mod}}</mod> <j>J</j>) — WebGL rendering, search, inline images, drag-drop paths and shell integration. Up to 10 sessions per project.
workspace.codeBody: A read-only file tree and Monaco viewer with plain-language AI summaries and “touched by AI” provenance chips, so anyone can follow what changed and why.
workspace.integrationsBody: A per-project marketplace of MCP integrations — Serena semantic code-navigation is bundled. Installs are additive: adding one never disturbs another.
workspace.themesBody: Five built-in themes — specrails (default), dracula, aurora-light, obsidian-dark and matrix — applied before the UI paints, so there's no flash. Change them in Settings → Appearance.
workspace.chatsBody: Park an Explore or AI-Edit session into a dock chip and pick it back up later — never lost across refreshes or project switches.
workspace.desktopBody: Run Specrails in your browser, or grab the signed desktop app (macOS & Windows) that bundles its own server and runtimes.

companion.title: Take Specrails with you
companion.subtitle: SpecRails Companion — your pipelines, on your phone
companion.intro: SpecRails Companion is the free mobile app for iPhone and Android that pairs with this desktop over your local network. Browse the specs board, launch and stop rails, follow live job logs and glance at analytics — straight from your phone.
companion.localBody: The app talks directly to this computer over your Wi-Fi — QR pairing with pinned TLS. No cloud relay, no account, and your code never leaves home.
companion.downloadHint: Scan a code with your phone's camera, or open the link.
companion.pairBody: Once installed, go to Settings → Mobile companion → Pair device and scan the QR code shown there.

moveFast.title: Move at the speed of thought
moveFast.subtitle: Keyboard-first, everywhere
moveFast.paletteBody: Switch projects, run spec commands, jump to any page and find recent jobs — all with fuzzy search.
moveFast.shortcutsBody: Press <q>?</q> to see the full cheat-sheet.
moveFast.projectsBody: Toggle the left Arc sidebar with <alt>{{alt}}</alt> <mod>{{mod}}</mod> <bkey>B</bkey>.
moveFast.outro: That's the tour. Add a project from the left sidebar and ship your first spec. You can reopen this walkthrough anytime from Settings.

jira.label: Jira integration
jira.body: Back any project with a Jira board — each spec becomes a Jira issue, kept in sync both ways: status follows the rail, and a comment is posted when a job finishes. Configure it per project when you add it, or anytime in Settings.
